notes.anchor: Since a day has been added to the games
notes.note: Public games could be prolonged by an added day, often for ritual or political reasons (an instauratio); the extra day gives Cicero leisure to spend with Dionysius.
C. Trebonius, tribune 55 BC, carried the lex Trebonia assigning the Spains to Pompey and Syria to Crassus for five years; Cicero and Atticus are weighing his conduct.
Cicero quotes a Greek proverb on perfect likeness ('no fig was ever so like a fig'), the oath 'by Demeter' being part of the tag, to liken Domitius's plight to his own.
L. Domitius Ahenobarbus, the leading anti-triumviral candidate, was being obstructed in his run for the consulship of 54 BC; Cicero pities him while noting his troubles are deserved.

Consul designatus: the title held between election and entry into office on 1 January. Cicero's barb is that Domitius has been a presumptive consul-in-waiting nearly all his life yet still cannot get elected.
A man from Firmum (modern Fermo) in Picenum, source of Cicero's information about the 'Firmian brothers'; otherwise unidentified.
